title: Mint-ChIP3: A low-input ChIP-seq protocol using multiplexed chromatin and T7 amplification
author: Lauren D Walter
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.wbefaje

steps.title: Suspend cells in 20 ul of...
steps.instruction: Suspend cells in 20 ul of ice cold PBS (typically 50K to 100K per antibody in use).

steps.title: Achieve lysis by adding 20 ul...
steps.instruction: Achieve lysis by adding 20 ul of 2X MNase/Lysis Mastermix, mix by using a few gentle cycles of pipetting.

steps.title: Hold on ice for 20 minutes [to achieve cell lysis]
steps.instruction: Hold on ice for 20 minutes [to achieve cell lysis].

steps.title: Transfer to a pre-warmed thermal cycler...
steps.instruction: Transfer to a pre-warmed thermal cycler at 37 o C for 10 minutes [to achieve MNase digestion].

steps.title: Immediately place samples on ice and add 4
steps.instruction: Immediately place samples on ice and add 4.8 uL of 0.5 M EGTA to each sample [to stop MNase activity]. In the Van Galen et al (

steps.title: protocol, EGTA was included in the ligation master mix
steps.instruction: protocol, EGTA was included in the ligation master mix. In contrast, we suggest you add EGTA alone at the conclusion of MNase digestion, as indicated.

steps.title: Use 1 uL of digested cells...
steps.instruction: Use 1 uL of digested cells to measure the DNA concentration with the dsDNA Qubit kit. A typical range in concentration for 50,000 cells / 40 uL lysate is 15 to 20 ng/ul. If you are multiplexing multiple chromatin preparations together, this measurement helps evaluate the equivalence (between lysates) of the number of cells in use. It is crucial that mixtures of chromatin have reasonably similar contributions from each component, because this drives the yield of ChIP DNA, the yield of library DNA, and the yield of reads from library sequencing. Imbalanced mixtures will lead to imbalanced yields from library sequencing. A further constraint on chromatin mixing can potentially arise in experiments involving treatment of cells with modulators of the activity of chromatin modifying enzymes, such as HDAC inhibitors. Chromatin from cells that have been extensively treated with such a drug cannot be combined with chromatin from untreated cells for the purpose of ChIP with an antibody recognizing the affected histone modification, when the global abundance of the modification has been highly perturbed by the treatment; in this case, the yield of ChIP DNA would also be correspondingly imbalanced between the treated and untreated samples. In effect, when multiplexing chromatin, you lose the opportunity to characterize the genomic landscape of the modification from a sample in which the modification is significantly less abundant -- either because there are many fewer cells, or because there are many fewer modifications per cell. T7 Adapter Ligation:

steps.title: On ice, add 4 uL of...
steps.instruction: On ice, add 4 uL of 250 uM annealed chromatin adapter to the cell lysate. The EGTA should protect the dsDNA adapters from residual MNase activity. If multiplexing distinct chromatin preparations together, be sure to use a different adapter barcode with each to enable subsequent demultiplexing. The following table summarizes 8 different barcodes we have found perform well; full adapter sequences and instructions for ordering are attached to this protocol. Suggested T7 Adapter barcodes.

steps.title: Add 31
steps.instruction: Add 31.8 uL of T7 Adapter Ligation Mastermix and immediately mix contents well. The total volume should be about 80 uL at this point.

steps.title: Cover the PCR plate with a...
steps.instruction: Cover the PCR plate with a foil cover, and incubate at room temperature for 2 hours [to ligate adapters to chromatin].

steps.title: After 2 hours, end the T7...
steps.instruction: After 2 hours, end the T7 Adapter Ligation reaction by adding 80 uL of the Ligase Quenching Solution (recipe above) to each sample. The total volume should be 160 uL at this point. The 160 uL is made up of cells, MNase/Lysis buffer mastermix, the T7 Adapter Ligation mastermix, and the Quenching solution. Chromatin Multiplexing (Optional) and ChIP Construction: Because each sample has a unique Mint Chromatin Adapter, multiple chromatin samples can optimally be mixed together at this point. Follow one of the following two procedures to complete Day

steps.title: a
steps.instruction: a. Mint-ChIP without Chromatin Multiplexing:

steps.title: If you are using an input...
steps.instruction: If you are using an input control in your experimental design, reserve 3% of the volume PRIOR to adding antibody. Hold the input control in a sealed tube at 4°C until Day 2 .

steps.title: Add 1-5 uL of antibody of...
steps.instruction: Add 1-5 uL of antibody of interest to each sample and incubate rotating end over end overnight at 4 o C. Depending on the antibody, the ideal volume of antibody to chromatin ratio will differ, and should be optimized empirically. We typically find that 1-2 ug of antibody works well for a range of antibodies and cell numbers. The sample can be divided prior to antibody addition, if using multiple antibodies, but typically if we have not combined multiple chromatin preps, then we don’t split the sample. If you are planning to split the sample, keep in mind the effective cell number per chromatin prep per ChIP at this point. We get excellent results with 50,000 to 100,000 cells per chromatin prep per antibody, and acceptable results with as few as 10,000 cells per antibody, depending on the antibody. If splitting a non-pooled sample (total volume = 160 ul), you may need to supplement the volume with a suitable buffer cocktail to enable gentle mixing during end-over-end rotation. We have standardized our operations to perform ChIPs in a minimum volume per ChIP of 100 ul, typically in a foil covered deep well plate or in individual microfuge tubes. Regardless of the plasticware used, as stated elsewhere, you must have a suitable method to magnetize the sample to wash the ChIPs on day two. If the chromatin volume is under 100 ul, we supplement the chromatin with a 1:1 mixture of Ligase Quenching Solution and 1X MNase/Lysis Mastermix with the MNase omitted . If no chromatin multiplexing is performed, and only a single chromatin adapter is in use, you will obtain a monotemplate (the T7 Adapter barcode) during the first 8 cycles of Illumina read

steps.title: We tend to avoid this by...
steps.instruction: We tend to avoid this by designing experiments with more than one chromatin adapter. If using only one adapter, you may be able to compensate by adding Illumna's PhiX174 control DNA during sequencing, and by using your Illumina flowcell at a lower loading density. b. Mint-ChIP With Chromatin Multiplexing:

steps.title: Mix up to 8 samples with...
steps.instruction: Mix up to 8 samples with distinct barcodes in a 1.5 ml microfuge tube (we typically use only 2 to 4 barcodes in a mixture).

steps.title: If you are using an input...
steps.instruction: If you are using an input control in your experimental design, reserve 3% of the mixture PRIOR to adding antibody. Hold the input control in a sealed tube at 4°C until Day 2 .

steps.title: Whatever the total volume of the...
steps.instruction: Whatever the total volume of the mixture, divide it evenly across the number of antibodies in use. Please see the comment above about the effective cell number per ChIP. In the case of a pooled sample, the relevant number is the total number of cells per chromatin prep per ChIP, not the total cell number over-all per ChIP. If the chromatin volume is under 100 ul, we supplement the chromatin with a 1:1 mixture of Ligase Quenching Solution and 1X MNase/Lysis Mastermix with the MNase omitted .

steps.title: Add 1-5 uL of antibody of...
steps.instruction: Add 1-5 uL of antibody of interest to each sample and incubate rotating end over end overnight at 4 o C. Depending on the antibody, the ideal volume of antibody to chromatin ratio will differ, as described above. The samples are typically in 1.5 ml microfuge tubes. You must have a suitable way to subject the tubes to a magnetic field, such as a Promega MagneSphere Magnetic Separation Stand, catalog # Z5342. ***This is the END of Day 1***

steps.title: Starting with chromatin-antibody complex and ending...
steps.instruction: Starting with chromatin-antibody complex and ending by set-up of In Vitro Transcription (IVT) Overview of Day

steps.title: Protein A/G Binding
steps.instruction: Protein A/G Binding

steps.title: In Vitro Transcription (Overnight)
steps.instruction: In Vitro Transcription (Overnight)

steps.title: Protein A/G Bead Binding:
steps.instruction: Protein A/G Bead Binding:

steps.title: Remove Protein A/G Magnetic Beads from...
steps.instruction: Remove Protein A/G Magnetic Beads from 4 o C and leave on rotator for ~30 minutes at room temperature so that beads are thoroughly and evenly mixed.

steps.title: Calculate total Protein A/G bead slurry needed
steps.instruction: Calculate total Protein A/G bead slurry needed. The total total slurry needed is equal to 1/10 of the total combined volume of the chromatin - immune complexes (i.e. 20uL per 200uL chromatin - antibody complex).

steps.title: Wash an appropriate volume of Protein...
steps.instruction: Wash an appropriate volume of Protein A/G beads with two volumes of lysis dilution buffer, by immobilizing beads on magnet, and removal of clear fluid. Then resuspend the washed beads in the same volume of lysis dilution buffer that was used to wash the beads.

steps.title: Add washed beads to each sample...
steps.instruction: Add washed beads to each sample (10 ul of reuspended beads per 100 ul of immune complex) and incubate on rotator for 1 hour at 4 o C.

steps.title: While the samples are incubating, supplement...
steps.instruction: While the samples are incubating, supplement ChIP Elution Buffer with Proteinase K (1.25 ul of 20 mg/ml Proteinase K per 100 ul of ChIP Elution Buffer; we call this CEBPK). You will require 100 ul of CEBPK per ChIP. Note, CEBPK tends to precipitate SDS if held on ice, so maintain at room temperature until used.

steps.title: ChIP Wash
steps.instruction: ChIP Wash: After the samples have incubated with Protein A/G beads for an hour, the ChIPs are ready to be washed. When washing, resuspend, magnetize, and aspirate as quickly as possible. Never leave for more than a minute.

steps.title: Magnetize samples and remove the supernatant (chromatin plus non-adsorbed antibodies)
steps.instruction: Magnetize samples and remove the supernatant (chromatin plus non-adsorbed antibodies). As a precaution you may save the supernatant as a backup at -20°C. Do not let samples dry while on magnet. Proceed immediately to ChIP wash.

steps.title: Pipette mix the beads with 200...
steps.instruction: Pipette mix the beads with 200 ul per well of ice cold RIPA Low Salt Buffer. Transfer the beads as suspended in Low Salt Buffer mixture to a 96 well plate. Take pains to minimize bead loss during transfer. Magnetize the plate and wait for the beads and Low Salt Buffer to separate, no more than 1 minute Remove and discard the supernatant once it is cleared. Remove the plate from the magnet Optional: Resuspend the beads in another 200 uL of ice cold RIPA Low Salt Buffer Magnetize the plate and wait for the beads and Low Salt Buffer to separate, no more than 1 minute Remove and discard the supernatant once it is cleared. Remove the plate from the magnet

steps.title: Mix the beads with 200 uL...
steps.instruction: Mix the beads with 200 uL of ice cold RIPA High Salt Buffer Magnetize the plate and wait for the beads and High Salt Buffer to separate Remove and discard the supernatant once it is cleared. Remove the plate from the magnet

steps.title: Mix the beads with 200 uL...
steps.instruction: Mix the beads with 200 uL of ice cold LiCl Wash Buffer Magnetize the plate and wait for the beads and LiCl Wash Buffer to separate Remove and discard the supernatant once it is cleared. Remove the plate from the magnet Optional: Resuspend the beads in another 200 uL of ice cold LiCl Wash Buffer Magnetize the plate and wait for the beads and LiCl Wash Buffer to separate, no more than 1 minute Remove and discard the supernatant once it is cleared.

steps.title: Mix the beads with 200 uL...
steps.instruction: Mix the beads with 200 uL of ice cold TE Magnetize the plate and wait for the beads and TE to separate Remove and discard the supernatant once it is cleared. Remove the plate from the magnet Resuspend the beads in another 200 uL of ice cold TE Buffer Magnetize the plate and wait for the beads and TE to separate, no more than 1 minute Remove and discard the supernatant once it is cleared.

steps.title: Resuspend the chromatin/antibody beads using 100...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the chromatin/antibody beads using 100 uL of room temperature CEBPK made earlier.

steps.title: At this point, we resume processing...
steps.instruction: At this point, we resume processing the input control chromatin (or chromatin mixture) that was reserved on Day 1 . Mix the reserved chromatin extract (typically about two microliters) with CEBPK to a total volume of 100 uL.

steps.title: Seal the plate with a foil...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate with a foil cover and hold at 62 o C for 1 hour in a thermocycler.

steps.title: While Proteinase K treatment is underway,...
steps.instruction: While Proteinase K treatment is underway, remove SPRI beads from 4°C and resuspend by rotating at room temperature for at least 30 minutes

steps.title: 1x SPRI Cleanup
steps.instruction: 1x SPRI Cleanup: At Room Temperature

steps.title: Remove the plate from the thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Remove the plate from the thermocycler and hold at room temperature

steps.title: Magnetize the plate and wait for...
steps.instruction: Magnetize the plate and wait for the beads and ChIP Elution Buffer to separate. a. Transfer the supernatant -- which contains your eluted ChIP DNA, and should be 100uL -- to new wells in the plate

steps.title: Remove the plate from the magnet...
steps.instruction: Remove the plate from the magnet and add 100 uL of suspended SPRI beads to your saved supernatant, to achieve a 1X SPRI cleanup. This should retain fragments over 100 BP in size. Mix the supernatant and SPRI beads by pipetting up and down several times Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes

steps.title: Place the plate on the magnet,...
steps.instruction: Place the plate on the magnet, loosely cover with the plastic cover from a pipette tip box, and magnetize for 5 minutes a. After 5 minutes, discard the supernatant, which represents sub-100 BP DNA fragments and diverse proteolysis byproducts.

steps.title: Wash with 200 uL of freshly...
steps.instruction: Wash with 200 uL of freshly prepared 75% ethanol (and always prepare fresh 75% ethanol for each experiment) Mix by moving the plate from the center of the magnet to off center of the magnet. You will see the beads move from one side of the well to the other side of the well. Leave on the magnet for 1 minute and then discard the ethanol.

steps.title: Wash AGAIN with 200 uL of...
steps.instruction: Wash AGAIN with 200 uL of 75% ethanol Mix by moving the plate from the center of the magnet to off center of the magnet. You will see the beads move from one side of the well to the other side of the well. Leave on the magnet for 1 minute and then discard the ethanol.

steps.title: Air-dry at room temperature for exactly...
steps.instruction: Air-dry at room temperature for exactly 5 minutes, and no longer; do not allow to over-dry.

steps.title: Elute DNA in 15 uL Elution buffer (EB)
steps.instruction: Elute DNA in 15 uL Elution buffer (EB). Remove plate from magnet and resuspend beads in 15 uL EB Let rest for 1 minute off the magnet and then 2 minutes on the magnet Once the beads are on the sides of the wells, remove and SAVE the supernatant; this is your purified ChIP DNA

steps.title: Use 2 uL for Qubit measurement,...
steps.instruction: Use 2 uL for Qubit measurement, to gain some insight into the success of your ChIP so far. We typically recover 0.50-2.00 ng/ul of ChIP DNA at this point. However, regardless of the ChIP DNA concentration, 8 ul of it will go into the IVT as shown in the box below. The remaining 5 ul is saved as a backup in case of a downstream failure.

steps.title: In Vitro Transcription (Overnight):
steps.instruction: In Vitro Transcription (Overnight):

steps.title: In a new plate, prepare the...
steps.instruction: In a new plate, prepare the following mixture of ChIP DNA and IVT Mastermix: T7 In vitro transcription master mix; total reaction volume is 20 ul per IVT.

steps.title: Cover plate with a foil cover...
steps.instruction: Cover plate with a foil cover and incubate at 37 o C for 16 hours (overnight). ***This is the END of Day 2***

steps.title: Starting with RNA Isolation and ending...
steps.instruction: Starting with RNA Isolation and ending with Library Construction Overview of Day

steps.title: RNA Isolation with Silane Beads
steps.instruction: RNA Isolation with Silane Beads

steps.title: Reverse Transcription (RNA to cDNA)
steps.instruction: Reverse Transcription (RNA to cDNA)

steps.title: Library PCR to Enrich Adapter-Modified DNA Fragments
steps.instruction: Library PCR to Enrich Adapter-Modified DNA Fragments

steps.title: Final SPRI Cleanup
steps.instruction: Final SPRI Cleanup

steps.title: RNA Isolation with Silane Beads:
steps.instruction: RNA Isolation with Silane Beads:

steps.title: Remove Silane Beads and SPRI Beads...
steps.instruction: Remove Silane Beads and SPRI Beads from 4°C and incubate on a rotator for at least 30 minutes at room temperature, or until beads are thoroughly and evenly mixed.

steps.title: Add 1 uL of DNase to...
steps.instruction: Add 1 uL of DNase to IVT RNA samples and incubate at 37°C for 15 minutes.

steps.title: Using a 96 well plate, magnetize...
steps.instruction: Using a 96 well plate, magnetize 20 uL of Silane Bead suspension per IVT reaction. Remove clear supernatant, and wash with four volumes of RLT Buffer (i.e. 80 uL RLT for 20 uL of beads), and resuspend washed beads in 4 volumes of RLT.

steps.title: Transfer 60 uL of the washed...
steps.instruction: Transfer 60 uL of the washed beads to the 20 uL IVT reaction. The 60 uL will contain 15 uL of beads which will bind up to 3 ug of RNA. The remaining 20 ul of beads is discarded. a. Incubate at room temperature for 1 minute

steps.title: Add 48 uL of 100% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 48 uL of 100% ethanol and mix well by pipetting a. Wait for 2.5 minutes, mix, and wait an additional 2.5 minutes (for a total of 5 minutes).

steps.title: Magnetize the plate a
steps.instruction: Magnetize the plate a. Remove and discard the supernatant once the solution looks clear. The RNA is adsorbed to the beads.

steps.title: Wash with 200 uL of FRESH 75% ethanol a
steps.instruction: Wash with 200 uL of FRESH 75% ethanol a. Mix by moving the plate from the center of the magnet to off center of the magnet. You will see the beads move from one side of the well to the other side of the well. b. Leave on the magnet for 1 minute and then remove the ethanol

steps.title: Wash AGAIN with 200 uL of FRESH 75% ethanol a
steps.instruction: Wash AGAIN with 200 uL of FRESH 75% ethanol a. Mix by moving the plate from the center of the magnet to off center of the magnet. You will see the beads move from one side of the well to the other side of the well. b. Leave on the magnet for 1 minute and then remove the ethanol c. It is critical to wash thoroughly to eliminate all DNase prior to the reverse transcription step.

steps.title: Air-dry at room temperature for 2 minutes
steps.instruction: Air-dry at room temperature for 2 minutes. Make sure not to over dry.

steps.title: Elute RNA with 9 uL TE a
steps.instruction: Elute RNA with 9 uL TE a. Remove plate from magnet and resuspend beads in 9 uL TE. Make sure to get all the beads off the sides of the wells b. Let rest for 1 minute off the magnet and then 2 minutes on the magnet c. Once the beads are on the sides of the wells, remove and SAVE the supernatant; it is your IVT RNA prep.

steps.title: OPTIONAL
steps.instruction: OPTIONAL: Measure 2 ul on RNA Qubit a. If below detection limit (4 ug/ml), the libraries are likely to be very low in complexity. b. Total amount of RNA should be higher than input DNA for IVT reaction; typically the yield of RNA is 50 ng to 100 ng.

steps.title: Reverse Transcription (RNA -> cDNA):
steps.instruction: Reverse Transcription (RNA -> cDNA):

steps.title: Combine IVT RNA with random primer...
steps.instruction: Combine IVT RNA with random primer and annealing buffer and incubate mixture at 65°C for 5 minutes on the thermocycler, then immediately cool to 4°C in the thermal cycler (i.e. ramp as fast as the instrument will allow).

steps.title: Once the program cools to 4...
steps.instruction: Once the program cools to 4 o C, remove the plate and place immediately on ice, for at least one minute.

steps.title: Supplement the primer annealed IVT RNA...
steps.instruction: Supplement the primer annealed IVT RNA with the master mix described below and return the plate to the thermal cycler. The thermal profile should be as follows: 10 minutes at 25 o C 50 minutes at 50 o C 5 minutes at 85 o C 4 o C indefinite. We employ a single PCR program with a pause in the middle to accomplish both the annealing and elongation steps.

steps.title: At the conclusion of the cDNA...
steps.instruction: At the conclusion of the cDNA synthesis step, add 1 uL RNase to each sample, mix thoroughly, and incubate at 37 o C for 15 minutes in a PCR machine, then return to 4C. At this point, you have eliminated the IVT RNA, and should only have cDNA.

steps.title: 1X SPRI Cleanup:
steps.instruction: 1X SPRI Cleanup:

steps.title: Add 68 uL of water to...
steps.instruction: Add 68 uL of water to cDNA so that the volume is easier to work with manually. The total volume is now 100 ul per well.

steps.title: Add 100 uL of SPRI Beads...
steps.instruction: Add 100 uL of SPRI Beads (suspended at the start of Day

steps.title: and mix by pipetting a
steps.instruction: and mix by pipetting a. Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes

steps.title: Magnetize plate for 5 minutes so...
steps.instruction: Magnetize plate for 5 minutes so that the beads separate out a. Remove and discard the supernatant

steps.title: Wash with 200 uL of FRESH 75% ethanol a
steps.instruction: Wash with 200 uL of FRESH 75% ethanol a. Mix by moving the plate from the center of the magnet to off center of the magnet. You will see the beads move from one side of the well to the other side of the well. b. Leave on the magnet for 1 minute and then remove the ethanol

steps.title: Wash AGAIN with 200 uL of FRESH 75% ethanol a
steps.instruction: Wash AGAIN with 200 uL of FRESH 75% ethanol a. Mix by moving the plate from the center of the magnet to off center of the magnet. You will see the beads move from one side of the well to the other side of the well. b. Leave on the magnet for 1 minute and then remove the ethanol

steps.title: Air-dry at room temperature for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Air-dry at room temperature for 5 minutes

steps.title: Elute cDNA in 24
steps.instruction: Elute cDNA in 24.5 uL TE a. Remove plate from magnet and resuspend beads in 24.5 uL TE. b. Let rest for 1 minute off the magnet and then 2 minutes on the magnet c. Once the beads are on the sides of the wells, remove and SAVE the supernatant; it is your cDNA.

steps.title: Use 1 ul of cDNA to...
steps.instruction: Use 1 ul of cDNA to measure cDNA concentration with ssDNA Qubit (this is not optional, as will be apparent immediately below).

steps.title: Library PCR to Enrich Adapter-Modified DNA Fragments
steps.instruction: Library PCR to Enrich Adapter-Modified DNA Fragments: We put a maximum of 200 ng of cDNA into the PCR, so if the yield of cDNA exceeds 200 ng, then the cDNA needs to be diluted prior to PCR. Using more than 200 ng input cDNA impairs the yield of DS DNA with suitable ends for Illumina sequencing. If the yield of cDNA is under 200 ng, we use the entire 200 ng volume in the PCR reaction. If the yield of cDNA exceeds 200 ng, remove the appropriate volume of cDNA (containing up to 200 ng) and add it to Elution Buffer so that the combined volume sums to 22.5 ul. Prepare the following PCR enrichment reaction and place on thermal cycler for the following times and temperatures: PCR Enrichment Reaction Components PCR Cycling Conditions

steps.title: Post PCR Final SPRI Cleanup:
steps.instruction: Post PCR Final SPRI Cleanup:

steps.title: Add 50 uL of molecular biology...
steps.instruction: Add 50 uL of molecular biology grade water and check that the volume is exactly 100 uL

steps.title: Add 60 uL of SPRI beads (0
steps.instruction: Add 60 uL of SPRI beads (0.60×) and mix by pipetting. The beads will bind DNA fragments of >700 bp. The purpose of this step is to remove these large fragments. a. Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes

steps.title: Magnetize plate for 5 minutes to...
steps.instruction: Magnetize plate for 5 minutes to separate the beads out a. After 5 minutes, transfer the supernatant (about 160 uL) to clean plate wells. The supernatant should contain DNA fragments of <700 bp. It is your library, now cleared of high molecular weight fragments.

steps.title: Add 35 uL of SPRI beads (0
steps.instruction: Add 35 uL of SPRI beads (0.95×) and mix by pipetting. The beads will bind to DNA fragments of >200 bp, thus excluding unincorporated PCR primers, which remain in solution. a. Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes

steps.title: Magnetize plate for 5 minutes to...
steps.instruction: Magnetize plate for 5 minutes to separate the beads out a. After 5 minutes, remove and discard the supernatant

steps.title: Wash with 200 uL of FRESH 75% ethanol a
steps.instruction: Wash with 200 uL of FRESH 75% ethanol a. Mix by moving the plate from the center of the magnet to off center of the magnet. You will see the beads move from one side of the well to the other side of the well. b. Leave on the magnet for 1 minute and then remove the ethanol

steps.title: Wash AGAIN with 200 uL of FRESH 75% ethanol a
steps.instruction: Wash AGAIN with 200 uL of FRESH 75% ethanol a. Mix by moving the plate from the center of the magnet to off center of the magnet. You will see the beads move from one side of the well to the other side of the well. b. Leave on the magnet for 1 minute and then remove the ethanol

steps.title: Air-dry at room temperature for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Air-dry at room temperature for 5 minutes

steps.title: Elute DNA in 40 uL Elution Buffer
steps.instruction: Elute DNA in 40 uL Elution Buffer. a. Remove plate from magnet and resuspend beads in 40 uL EB. b. Let rest for 1 minute off the magnet and then 2 minutes on the magnet c. Once the beads are on the sides of the wells, remove and SAVE the supernatant. The supernatant is your PCR product enriched for fragments in the size range of 200-700 bp.

steps.title: Analyze samples by dsDNA Qubit for...
steps.instruction: Analyze samples by dsDNA Qubit for concentration and BioAnalyzer for fragment size. An ideal BioAnalyzer trace is included here: An ideal BioAnalyzer trace showing the PCR product after SPRI bead cleanup. ***This is the END of Day 3***
